title: Unable to install podman on Ubuntu 18.04 LTS - Depends: crun (>= 100:0.17-4)
body:
/kind bug

**I have followed the instructions for installing on Ubuntu 18.04 [here](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation)**

```
sudo sh -c "echo 'kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1' > /etc/sysctl.d/00-local-userns.conf"
systemctl restart procps

# Use buster-backports on Debian 10 for a newer libseccomp2
sudo sh -c "echo 'deb http://deb.debian.org/debian buster-backports main' >> /etc/apt/sources.list"
sudo sh -c "echo 'deb https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/Debian_10/ /' > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list"
curl -L https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/Debian_10/Release.key | sudo apt-key add -
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 04EE7237B7D453EC
sudo apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 648ACFD622F3D138
sudo apt-get -y -t buster-backports install libseccomp2
sudo apt-get -y install podman
```
This is the output I get on install 
```
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
Some packages could not be installed. This may mean that you have
requested an impossible situation or if you are using the unstable
distribution that some required packages have not yet been created
or been moved out of Incoming.
The following information may help to resolve the situation:

The following packages have unmet dependencies:
 podman : Depends: crun (>= 100:0.17-4) but it is not going to be installed
          Recommends: fuse-overlayfs but it is not going to be installed
E: Unable to correct problems, you have held broken packages.
```

comments.body:
Hi @srix55 we stopped building podman for older LTS. So, I'd recommend you switch to Ubuntu 20.04 or newer or build from source. If you're interested in building a deb package yourself, you can find the debian packaging source at https://gitlab.com/rhcontainerbot/podman/-/tree/debian .

Hope that helps.
